narrative.headline: 2022 Toyota Tundra: the engine dominates owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 413 complaints with NHTSA. The engine is by far the most reported area at 38%, and powertrain-related reports pile on top of that (25%) along with the fuel/propulsion system (18%). No fires, injuries, or deaths are reported across the engine, powertrain, and fuel groups. The brakes draw the fewest complaints but carry the only injury (1) and a crash, so give them a careful look on a test drive.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: The single biggest complaint by a wide margin: 155 reports (38%). No crashes, fires, or injuries reported, but owners describe reduced engine power. Watch for power loss on a test drive.

issues.sample: The engine V6 turbo wastegate is defective. The problem causes a Reduced Engine Power. Toyota and its dealership are trying to hide the problem from consumer. This vehicle is with Michaels Toyota of Bellevue in Washington. There are numerous YouTube buyer with the same problem. T
issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 102 reports (25%), with 1 crash and no fires or injuries. Closely related to the engine complaints above.

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: 73 reports (18%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Owners mention stalling and check-engine warnings.

issues.title: Engine And Engine Cooling
issues.blurb: 36 reports (9%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Several reference NHTSA recall campaigns and parts availability.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: The fewest reports at 31 (8%) but the only injury in this list, plus 1 crash. Owners describe automatic braking activating with nothing ahead. Test the brakes thoroughly.
